arguments.task: Research Gorgias using only public web pages. Prefer official developer docs, API auth docs, pricing/access pages, changelogs, GitHub, MCP registry pages, and support docs. Find evidence for: one-line product description; available API authentication methods; whether API access is self-serve or gated/paid and why; API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth; rate limits; whether an official or public MCP server/integration exists; and any notable buildability signals. Return concise notes with source URLs for each finding, and stop at public pages only if login walls appear.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Gorgias sources and adjacent public pages.

raw_result.description: Customer support helpdesk with AI agent features for ecommerce brands.
raw_result.auth_notes: Developer docs indicate private apps authenticate with API keys/access tokens, while public apps use OAuth 2.0. Evidence gathered did not fully clarify whether 'access tokens' should be classified separately from api_key in this schema, so auth classification is normalized to api_key + oauth2.
raw_result.gating_reason: Private apps appear self-serve, but public app distribution in the Gorgias App Store requires review/approval; because toolkit-style integrations commonly target reusable/public app setups, access gating is at least partially approval-based.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer docs describe a RESTful, resource-oriented JSON API with broad helpdesk coverage including tickets, customers, messages, users, teams, macros, rules, tags, views, statistics, voice calls, widgets, files, jobs, events, and related account resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official or clearly public Gorgias MCP server was verified from the gathered evidence. This remains uncertain rather than definitively absent.

raw_result.blocker: A first private-app toolkit looks feasible from public REST docs, but reusable/public app distribution appears review-gated and MCP availability was not verified.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Developer docs note leaky-bucket rate limiting. Gathered evidence indicates OAuth2 apps get 80 requests per 20 seconds, API-key apps get 40 requests per 20 seconds, with tighter limits for some enterprise contexts.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer portal exists with API reference, authentication docs, changelog/quickstart navigation, and broad endpoint categories. Homepage messaging positions Gorgias as a helpdesk plus AI agent product.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Access tier is mixed: private apps seem self-serve while public apps require review. MCP existence was not conclusively verified, and auth normalization from 'API key/access token' into the allowed schema may need human confirmation.
